sources.label: Franklin Electric news search (Yahoo Finance quote/press releases; Q1 2026 results, reaffirmed FY26 guidance)
Simply Wall St - FELE valuation after reaffirmed 2026 guidance (May 11, 2026; PT nudged to $113.25)
Oppenheimer Conference recap - FELE standing up data-center liquid-cooling production at Oklahoma facility (May 5, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already corrected the rank up to reflect the adjusted score; nothing material has hit since June 6 and today's +2.1% is drift, not new evidence. The latent-node thesis (water/pumping for water-constrained compute) is quietly strengthening via the Oklahoma liquid-cooling production ramp, but that disclosure predates June 6, so hold with a small positive drift.
verdicts.bull: Company is standing up dedicated data-center liquid-cooling production capacity and management explicitly frames AI data centers as a strong adjacency, while the stock is only +5.4% YTD - latent AI exposure not yet priced.
verdicts.neutral: A $4.35B industrial pump maker with reaffirmed FY26 guidance ($2.17-2.24B sales) and Q1 sales +10%; data-center revenue is still a small adjacency, so the name trades on groundwater/fueling cycles for now.
verdicts.bear: Liquid-cooling is a crowded field (Vertiv, nVent, Boyd, etc.) and FELE's residential/ag groundwater core is rate- and weather-sensitive; a small insider sale and modest analyst PT (~$113) suggest limited near-term torque if the AI adjacency stays immaterial.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: First disclosed data-center liquid-cooling orders or revenue contribution from the Oklahoma facility, likely at Q2 earnings (late July).
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI cooling adjacency never scales beyond pilot volumes while core groundwater demand softens, leaving the latent-node thesis unmonetized.
